behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is request handoff between classifier module and API client payload construction. A unit test of a helper could miss wiring bugs where the sent payload omits labels. Real HTTP is not required; an in-process fake client can assert the outgoing request body. Rejected higher level (System): behavior text only requires that the request "sent" contains labels, not real network behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier module with a fake API client that records outbound payloads. Call classify() with a taxonomy and assert the captured request includes every taxonomy label, with no omissions, before client invocation returns.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This is local normalization/validation logic: mapping out-of-taxonomy label -> "unknown". The failure can be reproduced by feeding a raw label into normalization without module wiring, network, or model output quality checks.
behaviors.test_description: Test normalization function (or equivalent local mapper) with taxonomy {legal, technical, financial, other} and raw label "medical"; assert final label is exactly "unknown".
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: If implementation does not expose normalization directly, keep same assertion at module boundary with a stubbed response but the logical minimum remains Unit.

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly scopes to normalization determinism for identical inputs, which is pure local logic. No cross-module interaction is required to expose the failure.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization twice (or many times) with the exact same raw API response fixture and taxonomy; assert output label is identical across runs.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior requires repeated live calls and depends on real model/provider behavior for label consistency over time. A stub would hide nondeterminism and provider-side drift. Rejected higher level (Workflow): although repeated calls occur, this is not a multi-stage agent pipeline with sequential model decisions handing off state; it is repeated evaluation of one agentic step.
behaviors.test_description: Run N repeated real classification calls for identical (document, taxonomy), recording provider raw response and final label each time. Assert label is stable whenever provider response content is unchanged; allow differences only when provider response changes.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module boundary between token-budget policy enforcement and dispatch control (truncate/reject and whether call is sent). Needs several local modules wired; no real OS/network/model output required. Rejected higher level (System): behavior text is specifically "before dispatch," so in-process fake client is sufficient to detect blocked/modified dispatch.
behaviors.test_description: Configure budget and policy, inject fake tokenizer + fake API client. For oversized input, assert module applies configured truncate/reject policy and only dispatches when policy permits; verify dispatched payload conforms to budget gate decisions.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real provider-reported usage from an external AI API call; mocks can hide tokenization/accounting differences that only appear with the live model/provider. Even with deterministic assertions, correctness depends on real AI API behavior in execution path. Rejected lower Integration: stub usage values would always reflect fixture assumptions and can miss real provider accounting drift.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real API calls on representative documents with configured budget, capture provider-reported token usage metadata, and assert observed usage does not exceed configured per-call budget (or triggers policy when near/exceeding).

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This is retry orchestration logic across classifier and API client interface. A fake client that returns rate-limit then success plus controllable clock/backoff can expose real logic failures. No real OS/network required, and no dependence on model output quality. Rejected higher System: behavior text concerns handling a rate-limit signal, which can be faithfully simulated in-process for minimum failure detection.
behaviors.test_description: Use fake API client scripted to return rate-limit response then success. Invoke classify() and assert module delays per retry policy, retries expected number of times, and ultimately returns successful label instead of surfacing immediate error.
